commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A maintainer needs to decide whether to accept the new harness command, job config keys, and opt-in compliance exit-code behavior; no narrow automated repair is identified.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue found; the risky local plan_file read is constrained to safe repo-relative paths and the earlier plugin-tool expansion is no longer in the diff. (sha=e1052da3f7e3e6ce7442de443ff22714ea256a5e)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds harness Markdown validation and `--harness`/`--index` support for `crabbox run` and `crabbox job run`, plus local/remote evidence, compliance reports, timing metadata, job config defaults, docs, and Go tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The contributor provided a high-confidence behavior proof path with real Islo `crabbox run --harness --timing-json` output for passing and compliance-failing runs.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer needs to decide whether to accept the new harness command, job config keys, and opt-in compliance exit-code behavior; no narrow automated repair is identified.

target.title: Add harness-aware run evidence
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds harness-aware evidence for `crabbox run` and `crabbox job run`: Markdown harness parsing, YAML frontmatter validation, plan/harness hashes, light grounding metadata, and local compliance reports.
- Adds `crabbox harness validate [--json] HARNESS.md` plus job config support for `harness.path` / `harness.index` defaults in `.crabbox.yaml`.
- Documents harness usage, evidence outputs, compliance behavior, and a generic Harness/Station/Reasoning Station roadmap.
- After merging latest `main`, this PR no longer includes root OpenClaw plugin tool additions; those can be reconsidered separately because main removed the root plugin package.

## What The New Pieces Do
- `internal/cli/harness.go`: parses harness Markdown/frontmatter, rejects unknown top-level and nested frontmatter keys, computes hashes, builds grounding JSON, writes remote/local evidence, and renders compliance reports.
- `run` / `job run`: accept `--harness` and `--index none|light`; harness metadata is included in timing JSON and compliance failures can make an otherwise successful command return nonzero.
- Delegated providers such as Islo emit the final harness-aware timing report from the CLI when a harness is attached.
- Compliance reports write `harness.md`, `grounding.json`, `compliance-report.md`, and `compliance-report.json` under `.crabbox/runs/<run-or-lease>/`.

## Review Fixes Included
- Fixed CLI flag examples/order so `--json` and `job run --harness` match the Go parsers.
- Fixed required artifact compliance so unrelated artifacts no longer satisfy all requirements, and JUnit evidence can satisfy `required_artifacts: junit` when results were parsed.
- Fixed compliance ordering so proof artifacts are produced before harness compliance is evaluated, allowing proof evidence to be required.
- Fixed delegated-run harness compliance so delegated execution errors cannot look like a passed harness because of a zero-valued result exit code.
- Fixed harness compliance finalization so a command that exits 0 but fails compliance is recorded in run history and timing JSON with the effective harness failure exit code.
- Fixed delegated-provider timing so Islo-style harness runs emit a single final harness-aware timing JSON report from the CLI.
- Simplified job-level `harness:` config to only `path` and `index`, the values that `job run` can forward to the nested `run`; plan/scope/compliance live in the harness file.
- Added safety boundaries: harness `plan_file` values must be safe repo-relative paths before Crabbox reads/hashes them.

## Verification

Local CI-equivalent verification after merging latest `main`:

```text
$ gofmt -l $(git ls-files '*.go')
# no output

$ go vet ./... && go test -race ./...
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/cmd/crabbox	2.153s
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/internal/cli	56.046s
...
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/internal/providers/wandb	11.777s

$ node --test scripts/*.test.js
ℹ tests 51
ℹ pass 51
ℹ fail 0

$ scripts/check-docs.sh
checked 42 command docs: command surface ok
checked 139 markdown files: internal links ok
built docs site: dist/docs-site

$ npm ci && npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run check && npm test && npm run build  # in worker/
All matched files use the correct format.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Test Files 14 passed (14)
Tests 316 passed (316)
wrangler deploy --dry-run completed successfully.
```

## Real Islo Runtime Proof

I used the installed `islo` CLI to create a temporary API key, ran two real `crabbox run --provider islo --harness ... --timing-json` invocations against a tiny throwaway git repo, then removed the local key file and deactivated/deleted the generated key. Sandbox ids, friendly slugs, and local temp paths are redacted below.

Passing harness run:

```text
$ crabbox run --provider islo --islo-image docker.io/library/ubuntu:26.04 \
  --harness HARNESS-pass.md --timing-json --label pr190-islo-pass -- \
  /bin/sh -c 'printf "islo harness pass\\n"'

stdout:
islo harness pass

stderr excerpt:
leased isb_<redacted> slug=<redacted> provider=islo sandbox=<redacted>
provider=islo lease=isb_<redacted> sandbox=<redacted>
sync candidate: 4 files, 296 B
sync complete in 1.08s
islo run summary sync=1.08s command=478ms total=56.507s exit=0
artifact kind=harness path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md bytes=131
artifact kind=grounding path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json bytes=642
artifact kind=compliance-json path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json bytes=413
artifact kind=compliance-report path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md bytes=256
{"provider":"islo","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":0,"label":"pr190-islo-pass","harness":{"path":"HARNESS-pass.md","template":"smoke","planHash":"<redacted>","index":"light","status":"passed"}}
```

Compliance-failing harness run (remote command exits 0, harness fails because required evidence is absent):

```text
$ crabbox run --provider islo --islo-image docker.io/library/ubuntu:26.04 \
  --harness HARNESS-fail.md --timing-json --label pr190-islo-fail -- \
  /bin/sh -c 'printf "islo harness compliance fail\\n"'

stdout:
islo harness compliance fail

stderr excerpt:
leased isb_<redacted> slug=<redacted> provider=islo sandbox=<redacted>
provider=islo lease=isb_<redacted> sandbox=<redacted>
sync candidate: 4 files, 296 B
sync complete in 1.179s
islo run summary sync=1.179s command=438ms total=53.306s exit=0
artifact kind=harness path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md bytes=144
artifact kind=grounding path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json bytes=619
artifact kind=compliance-json path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json bytes=404
artifact kind=compliance-report path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md bytes=231
{"provider":"islo","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":8,"label":"pr190-islo-fail","blockedStage":"harness-compliance","retryLikely":"unknown","harness":{"path":"HARNESS-fail.md","template":"smoke","index":"light","status":"failed"}}
harness compliance failed
```

Evidence files produced by the Islo run:

```text
.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md
.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json
```

## Notes
- Harnesses deliberately do not add LLM judging, vector indexes, scenario databases, autonomous state machines, or a remote daemon.
- Agent/plugin exposure should be revisited separately after the root plugin package removal on `main` settles.
target.author: zozo123

```text
{"provider":"islo","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":8,"label":"pr190-islo-fail","blockedStage":"harness-compliance","retryLikely":"unknown","harness":{"path":"HARNESS-fail.md","template":"smoke","index":"light","status":"failed"}}
harness compliance failed
```

Evidence files produced by the Islo run:

```text
.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md
.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json
```

## Notes
- Harnesses deliberately do not add LLM judging, vector indexes, scenario databases, autonomous state machines, or a remote daemon.
- Agent/plugin exposure should be revisited separately after the root plugin package removal on `main` settles.

commands.author: zozo123

target.title: Add harness-aware run evidence
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds harness-aware evidence for `crabbox run` and `crabbox job run`: Markdown harness parsing, YAML frontmatter validation, plan/harness hashes, light grounding metadata, and local compliance reports.
- Adds `crabbox harness validate [--json] HARNESS.md` plus job config support for `harness.path` / `harness.index` defaults in `.crabbox.yaml`.
- Documents harness usage, evidence outputs, compliance behavior, and a generic Harness/Station/Reasoning Station roadmap.
- After merging latest `main`, this PR no longer includes root OpenClaw plugin tool additions; those can be reconsidered separately because main removed the root plugin package.

## What The New Pieces Do
- `internal/cli/harness.go`: parses harness Markdown/frontmatter, rejects unknown top-level and nested frontmatter keys, computes hashes, builds grounding JSON, writes remote/local evidence, and renders compliance reports.
- `run` / `job run`: accept `--harness` and `--index none|light`; harness metadata is included in timing JSON and compliance failures can make an otherwise successful command return nonzero.
- Delegated providers such as Islo emit the final harness-aware timing report from the CLI when a harness is attached.
- Compliance reports write `harness.md`, `grounding.json`, `compliance-report.md`, and `compliance-report.json` under `.crabbox/runs/<run-or-lease>/`.

## Review Fixes Included
- Fixed CLI flag examples/order so `--json` and `job run --harness` match the Go parsers.
- Fixed required artifact compliance so unrelated artifacts no longer satisfy all requirements, and JUnit evidence can satisfy `required_artifacts: junit` when results were parsed.
- Fixed compliance ordering so proof artifacts are produced before harness compliance is evaluated, allowing proof evidence to be required.
- Fixed delegated-run harness compliance so delegated execution errors cannot look like a passed harness because of a zero-valued result exit code.
- Fixed harness compliance finalization so a command that exits 0 but fails compliance is recorded in run history and timing JSON with the effective harness failure exit code.
- Fixed delegated-provider timing so Islo-style harness runs emit a single final harness-aware timing JSON report from the CLI.
- Simplified job-level `harness:` config to only `path` and `index`, the values that `job run` can forward to the nested `run`; plan/scope/compliance live in the harness file.
- Added safety boundaries: harness `plan_file` values must be safe repo-relative paths before Crabbox reads/hashes them.

## Verification

Local CI-equivalent verification after merging latest `main`:

```text
$ gofmt -l $(git ls-files '*.go')
# no output

$ go vet ./... && go test -race ./...
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/cmd/crabbox	2.153s
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/internal/cli	56.046s
...
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/internal/providers/wandb	11.777s

$ node --test scripts/*.test.js
ℹ tests 51
ℹ pass 51
ℹ fail 0

$ scripts/check-docs.sh
checked 42 command docs: command surface ok
checked 139 markdown files: internal links ok
built docs site: dist/docs-site

$ npm ci && npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run check && npm test && npm run build  # in worker/
All matched files use the correct format.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Test Files 14 passed (14)
Tests 316 passed (316)
wrangler deploy --dry-run completed successfully.
```

## Real Islo Runtime Proof

I used the installed `islo` CLI to create a temporary API key, ran two real `crabbox run --provider islo --harness ... --timing-json` invocations against a tiny throwaway git repo, then removed the local key file and deactivated/deleted the generated key. Sandbox ids, friendly slugs, and local temp paths are redacted below.

Passing harness run:

```text
$ crabbox run --provider islo --islo-image docker.io/library/ubuntu:26.04 \
  --harness HARNESS-pass.md --timing-json --label pr190-islo-pass -- \
  /bin/sh -c 'printf "islo harness pass\\n"'

stdout:
islo harness pass

stderr excerpt:
leased isb_<redacted> slug=<redacted> provider=islo sandbox=<redacted>
provider=islo lease=isb_<redacted> sandbox=<redacted>
sync candidate: 4 files, 296 B
sync complete in 1.08s
islo run summary sync=1.08s command=478ms total=56.507s exit=0
artifact kind=harness path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md bytes=131
artifact kind=grounding path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json bytes=642
artifact kind=compliance-json path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json bytes=413
artifact kind=compliance-report path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md bytes=256
{"provider":"islo","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":0,"label":"pr190-islo-pass","harness":{"path":"HARNESS-pass.md","template":"smoke","planHash":"<redacted>","index":"light","status":"passed"}}
```

Compliance-failing harness run (remote command exits 0, harness fails because required evidence is absent):

```text
$ crabbox run --provider islo --islo-image docker.io/library/ubuntu:26.04 \
  --harness HARNESS-fail.md --timing-json --label pr190-islo-fail -- \
  /bin/sh -c 'printf "islo harness compliance fail\\n"'

stdout:
islo harness compliance fail

stderr excerpt:
leased isb_<redacted> slug=<redacted> provider=islo sandbox=<redacted>
provider=islo lease=isb_<redacted> sandbox=<redacted>
sync candidate: 4 files, 296 B
sync complete in 1.179s
islo run summary sync=1.179s command=438ms total=53.306s exit=0
artifact kind=harness path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md bytes=144
artifact kind=grounding path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json bytes=619
artifact kind=compliance-json path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json bytes=404
artifact kind=compliance-report path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md bytes=231
{"provider":"islo","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":8,"label":"pr190-islo-fail","blockedStage":"harness-compliance","retryLikely":"unknown","harness":{"path":"HARNESS-fail.md","template":"smoke","index":"light","status":"failed"}}
harness compliance failed
```

Evidence files produced by the Islo run:

```text
.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md
.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json
```

## Notes
- Harnesses deliberately do not add LLM judging, vector indexes, scenario databases, autonomous state machines, or a remote daemon.
- Agent/plugin exposure should be revisited separately after the root plugin package removal on `main` settles.


Evidence files produced by the Islo run:

```text
.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md
.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json
```

## Notes
- Harnesses deliberately do not add LLM judging, vector indexes, scenario databases, autonomous state machines, or a remote daemon.
- Agent/plugin exposure should be revisited separately after the root plugin package removal on `main` settles.
target.author: zozo123

commands.author: zozo123

target.title: Add harness-aware run evidence
target.body:
## Summary
- Adds harness-aware evidence for `crabbox run` and `crabbox job run`: Markdown harness parsing, YAML frontmatter validation, plan/harness hashes, light grounding metadata, and local compliance reports.
- Adds `crabbox harness validate [--json] HARNESS.md` plus job config support for `harness.path` / `harness.index` defaults in `.crabbox.yaml`.
- Documents harness usage, evidence outputs, compliance behavior, and a generic Harness/Station/Reasoning Station roadmap.
- After merging latest `main`, this PR no longer includes root OpenClaw plugin tool additions; those can be reconsidered separately because main removed the root plugin package.

## What The New Pieces Do
- `internal/cli/harness.go`: parses harness Markdown/frontmatter, rejects unknown top-level and nested frontmatter keys, computes hashes, builds grounding JSON, writes remote/local evidence, and renders compliance reports.
- `run` / `job run`: accept `--harness` and `--index none|light`; harness metadata is included in timing JSON and compliance failures can make an otherwise successful command return nonzero.
- Delegated providers such as Islo emit the final harness-aware timing report from the CLI when a harness is attached.
- Compliance reports write `harness.md`, `grounding.json`, `compliance-report.md`, and `compliance-report.json` under `.crabbox/runs/<run-or-lease>/`.

## Review Fixes Included
- Fixed CLI flag examples/order so `--json` and `job run --harness` match the Go parsers.
- Fixed required artifact compliance so unrelated artifacts no longer satisfy all requirements, and JUnit evidence can satisfy `required_artifacts: junit` when results were parsed.
- Fixed compliance ordering so proof artifacts are produced before harness compliance is evaluated, allowing proof evidence to be required.
- Fixed delegated-run harness compliance so delegated execution errors cannot look like a passed harness because of a zero-valued result exit code.
- Fixed harness compliance finalization so a command that exits 0 but fails compliance is recorded in run history and timing JSON with the effective harness failure exit code.
- Fixed delegated-provider timing so Islo-style harness runs emit a single final harness-aware timing JSON report from the CLI.
- Simplified job-level `harness:` config to only `path` and `index`, the values that `job run` can forward to the nested `run`; plan/scope/compliance live in the harness file.
- Added safety boundaries: harness `plan_file` values must be safe repo-relative paths before Crabbox reads/hashes them.

## Verification

Local CI-equivalent verification after merging latest `main`:

```text
$ gofmt -l $(git ls-files '*.go')
# no output

$ go vet ./... && go test -race ./...
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/cmd/crabbox	2.153s
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/internal/cli	56.046s
...
ok  	github.com/openclaw/crabbox/internal/providers/wandb	11.777s

$ node --test scripts/*.test.js
ℹ tests 51
ℹ pass 51
ℹ fail 0

$ scripts/check-docs.sh
checked 42 command docs: command surface ok
checked 139 markdown files: internal links ok
built docs site: dist/docs-site

$ npm ci && npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run check && npm test && npm run build  # in worker/
All matched files use the correct format.
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
Test Files 14 passed (14)
Tests 316 passed (316)
wrangler deploy --dry-run completed successfully.
```

## Real Islo Runtime Proof

I used the installed `islo` CLI to create a temporary API key, ran two real `crabbox run --provider islo --harness ... --timing-json` invocations against a tiny throwaway git repo, then removed the local key file and deactivated/deleted the generated key. Sandbox ids, friendly slugs, and local temp paths are redacted below.

Passing harness run:

```text
$ crabbox run --provider islo --islo-image docker.io/library/ubuntu:26.04 \
  --harness HARNESS-pass.md --timing-json --label pr190-islo-pass -- \
  /bin/sh -c 'printf "islo harness pass\\n"'

stdout:
islo harness pass

stderr excerpt:
leased isb_<redacted> slug=<redacted> provider=islo sandbox=<redacted>
provider=islo lease=isb_<redacted> sandbox=<redacted>
sync candidate: 4 files, 296 B
sync complete in 1.08s
islo run summary sync=1.08s command=478ms total=56.507s exit=0
artifact kind=harness path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md bytes=131
artifact kind=grounding path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json bytes=642
artifact kind=compliance-json path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json bytes=413
artifact kind=compliance-report path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md bytes=256
{"provider":"islo","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":0,"label":"pr190-islo-pass","harness":{"path":"HARNESS-pass.md","template":"smoke","planHash":"<redacted>","index":"light","status":"passed"}}
```

Compliance-failing harness run (remote command exits 0, harness fails because required evidence is absent):

```text
$ crabbox run --provider islo --islo-image docker.io/library/ubuntu:26.04 \
  --harness HARNESS-fail.md --timing-json --label pr190-islo-fail -- \
  /bin/sh -c 'printf "islo harness compliance fail\\n"'

stdout:
islo harness compliance fail

stderr excerpt:
leased isb_<redacted> slug=<redacted> provider=islo sandbox=<redacted>
provider=islo lease=isb_<redacted> sandbox=<redacted>
sync candidate: 4 files, 296 B
sync complete in 1.179s
islo run summary sync=1.179s command=438ms total=53.306s exit=0
artifact kind=harness path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md bytes=144
artifact kind=grounding path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json bytes=619
artifact kind=compliance-json path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json bytes=404
artifact kind=compliance-report path=<tmp>/.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md bytes=231
{"provider":"islo","syncDelegated":true,"exitCode":8,"label":"pr190-islo-fail","blockedStage":"harness-compliance","retryLikely":"unknown","harness":{"path":"HARNESS-fail.md","template":"smoke","index":"light","status":"failed"}}
harness compliance failed
```

Evidence files produced by the Islo run:

```text
.crabbox/runs/run/harness.md
.crabbox/runs/run/grounding.json
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.md
.crabbox/runs/run/compliance-report.json
```

## Notes
- Harnesses deliberately do not add LLM judging, vector indexes, scenario databases, autonomous state machines, or a remote daemon.
- Agent/plugin exposure should be revisited separately after the root plugin package removal on `main` settles.
